justification: All pass criteria met: conflict between 100ms consistency and 30s CDN TTL explicitly named as a '300x violation'; all three agents' positions attributed (Bird's 100ms rule, MJ's 30s CDN for scalability, Pippen's operational CDN requirement); escalation routed to Coach K with 4 concrete and distinct resolution options (relax rule for passive viewers, short TTL with instant purge, WebSocket push, tiered model); no brief produced asking Shaq to implement both constraints — instead the output blocks implementation pending Coach K resolution.

observations.text: Exceeded the minimum 3 resolution paths by providing 4 distinct, concrete options with clear tradeoff analysis
Included a recommendation (Option A) while correctly noting Bird must approve as domain authority
Low confidence score (35) is well-calibrated — the technical contradiction is clear but the resolution requires human/domain judgment
Explicitly flags assumptions that need verification, e.g. whether the 100ms rule applies to all readers or just the scoring player
Correctly characterizes the conflict magnitude ('300x violation') making the incompatibility unambiguous

agent_output_excerpt: {"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"Bird requires leaderboard rankings to reflect score changes within 100ms with no stale reads. MJ's architecture routes all reads through a Fastly CDN cache with a 30-second TTL. Pippen confirms this means reads can be up to 30 seconds stale — a 300x violation of Bird's 100ms freshness rule.
